name: SentencePiece (Unsupervised Text Tokenizer for NLP)

performance.notes: C++ backend; extremely fast tokenization; microsecond per-token latency

agent_readiness.notes: Unsupervised tokenizer for NLP — BPE and unigram language model subword segmentation. import sentencepiece as spm. spm.SentencePieceTrainer.train(input='corpus.txt', model_prefix='tokenizer', vocab_size=32000, model_type='bpe'). sp = spm.SentencePieceProcessor(model_file='tokenizer.model'). sp.encode('Hello world', out_type=str). sp.decode([1,2,3]). sp.get_piece_size() for vocab size. model_type: bpe, unigram, char, word. Used by T5, ALBERT, XLNet, LLaMA tokenizers. Language-agnostic — no whitespace assumption. Handles CJK, multilingual text. Byte fallback for unknown chars.

gotchas: Training requires large text corpus for quality tokenization
Model file must be distributed alongside model weights
Documentation is thin — mostly README and examples in GitHub
Vocabulary size choice has significant impact on model quality
best_when: Training custom LLM tokenizers for domain-specific vocabularies
Multilingual NLP where language-agnostic subword is required
Loading pre-trained model tokenizers (LLaMA, T5, ALBERT use it)
When reproducible, language-agnostic tokenization is needed
avoid_when: Using HuggingFace models (use transformers tokenizers which may use this internally)
Simple English-only tokenization (NLTK or spaCy sufficient)
When a pre-trained tokenizer already exists for your task
summary: SentencePiece provides language-agnostic subword tokenization (BPE and unigram LM). Train: SentencePieceTrainer.train(input='corpus.txt', model_prefix='tok', vocab_size=32000). Encode/decode: sp.encode('text'); sp.decode(ids). C++ backend — microsecond tokenization. Used by LLaMA, T5, ALBERT, XLNet. Language-agnostic (no whitespace assumption) — excellent for CJK and multilingual. Apache-2.0 by Google. Best for custom tokenizer training for LLMs and multilingual NLP.
